assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 7 contains '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' which exactly matches the expected values: Role='Rust backend service', Serena Instance='serena_backend', Path='/home/user/trustify-backend'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 8 contains '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — a new trustify-ui entry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md explicitly labels entries with Status column: trustify-backend is 'Preserved' (line 11), trustify-ui is 'Added' (line 12). Jira fields are all marked 'Preserved' (lines 19-23). Code Intelligence entries show 'Preserved' for existing and 'Added' for serena_ui limitation (lines 30-32). Bug Configuration fields are all 'Preserved' (lines 38-40).

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 9-10: '1. **serena_backend** — Already configured in Repository Registry. No action needed.' and '2. **serena_ui** — Newly discovered. Not present in existing Repository Registry.'

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 20-33 preserve the naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.'), the example (mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol call), and the serena_backend limitation ('rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). The only addition is line 34: '- `serena_ui`: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 36-41 show the Bug Configuration section preserved with: Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks. changes-log.md line 42 states 'No changes made. assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output

User declined. No Security Configuration section added.' changes-log.md lines 45-46 confirm: 'Not added. User declined to enable security triage.'
